description: PhlF-repressible promoter: J23119-derived core with the phO operator overlapping the -10, so PhlF binding blocks transcription

references.authors: Stanton BC, Nielsen AA, Tamsir A, Clancy K, Peterson T, Voigt CA
references.title: Genomic mining of prokaryotic repressors for orthogonal logic gates

references.authors: Nielsen AA, Der BS, Shin J, Vaidyanathan P, Paralanov V, Strychalski EA, Ross D, Densmore D, Voigt CA
references.title: Genetic circuit design automation

references.authors: Abbas A, Morrissey JP, Marquez PC, Sheehan MM, Delany IR, O'Gara F
references.title: Characterization of interactions between the transcriptional repressor PhlF and its binding site at the phlA promoter in Pseudomonas fluorescens F113

label: PphlF
note: PhlF-repressible promoter: J23119-derived core with the phO operator overlapping the -10, so PhlF binding blocks transcription.
-10 box (TATCGT); overlapped by the phO operator

label: phO
note: PhlF operator (phO): 30-bp PhlF-dimer binding site (inverted repeat). Native operator defined by Abbas 2002 (downstream of the phlA TSS); overlaid onto the -10 in the synthetic PphlF (Stanton 2014) so PhlF binding occludes transcription.

functional_claims.label: PhlF repression of PphlF is relieved by 2,4-diacetylphloroglucinol (DAPG).

source.quote: Repression is relieved by the small-molecule inducer 2,4-diacetylphloroglucinol (DAPG).

functional_claims.label: PhlF gives approximately 80-fold repression of PphlF in the source work.

source.quote: PhlF gives high-fold repression (~80x in the source work), induced by DAPG.
